popularStory.description: Sir William fought in the Battle of Agincourt in France with King Henry V, becoming a knight Bannerett. He was widely known in South Wales and was buried in the church at St. Mary's

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG於1401年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾拉格藍時，他的父親Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert45歲，母親Maud de Morley32歲。他於1421年與Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam在英國賀福爾布雷德沃迪結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他於1442年被列為chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales。他去世於1446年在威爾士蒙姆爾阿加文尼享年45歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆易斯勒哈姆 普利奧利 邱奇。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG于1401年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾拉格藍时，他的父亲Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert45岁，母亲Maud de Morley32岁。他于1421年与Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam在英国賀福爾布雷德沃迪结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他于1442年被列为chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales。他去世于1446年在威尔士蒙姆爾阿加文尼享年45岁，埋葬在英国英格兰坎布利其爾易斯勒哈姆易斯勒哈姆 普利奧利 邱奇。

nameMeanings.name: Herbert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German, English, and French: from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements heri, hari ‘army’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. In Britain, this Old French name, introduced by the Normans, reinforced the less common Old English cognate Herebeorht. The surname was taken to Ireland after the Anglo-Norman invasion and in the 16th century.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): from the German personal name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Harbison
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name Herbert (the change from -er- to -ar- was a common one in Old French and Middle English). An intrusive n later gave Harbinson .

variants.name: Hebard
meanings: English (Surrey):
variant of Herbert , with assimilation of the first r. Compare Hebb .
variant of   Hibbert  .

variants.name: Harbert
meanings: German and English: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbers
meanings: German and Dutch: patronymic from   Herbert  . It is found mainly in Lower Saxony and across the Dutch border.

variants.name: Dickherber
meanings: Probably a nickname for a fat person by the name of Herbert , of which Herber is a short form, or, more likely, in the north from Die(c)kherber, a topographic and distinguishing name for a Herber living by the dike, see Dicke 2. Compare Dickmeyer .

variants.name: Hebert
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Armand, Andre, Emile, Normand, Jacques, Marcel, Pierre, Lucien, Michel, Gaston, Alcide, Camille.
French (Hébert): assimilated form of Herbert . Compare Abair , Abar , Abare , Abear , Aber , Eber , and Ebert .
German: variant of   Heber   1.

variants.name: Herber
meanings: German and Dutch: variant of   Herbert  .

variants.name: Herbertson
meanings: Scottish (Glasgow): patronymic from the Older Scots and Middle English personal name   Herbert  .

variants.name: Hebb
meanings: English (East Yorkshire and northeast Midlands): from the Middle English personal name Hebb, Ebb, pet forms of Herbert .

variants.name: Herb
meanings: South German: nickname for a harsh or rough person, from Middle High German herb ‘tart, harsh’.
German: from the personal name Herpo, a pet form of Herbert .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of any of the names beginning with this syllable.

variants.name: Alexander
meanings: From the Latin form of the Greek name Alexandros, from alexein ‘to defend’ + anēr ‘man, warrior’ (genitive andros). The name became extremely popular in the post-classical period, and was borne by several individuals in the New Testament and some early Christian saints. Its use as a common given name throughout Europe, however, derives largely from the fame of Alexander the Great , King of Macedon ( 356–323 bc ), around whom a large body of popular legend grew up in late antiquity, much of which came to be embodied in the medieval ‘Alexander romances’.

variants.name: Alan
meanings: Of Celtic origin and uncertain derivation (possibly a diminutive of a word meaning ‘rock’). It was introduced into England by Breton followers of William the Conqueror , most notably Alan , Earl of Brittany, who was rewarded for his services with vast estates in the newly conquered kingdom. In Britain the variants Allan and Allen are considerably less frequent, and generally represent transferred uses of surname forms, whereas in America all three forms of the name are approximately equally common. See also Alun .

variants.name: Albert
meanings: From an Old French name, Albert, of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from adal ‘noble’ + berht ‘bright, famous’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, displacing the Old English form Æþelbeorht. The name is popular in a variety of forms in Western Europe, and has been traditional in a number of European princely families. It was out of favour in England for centuries, however, and the revival of its popularity in the 19th century was largely in honour of Queen Victoria's consort, Prince Albert of Saxe-Coburg-Gotha .

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG was born in 1401, in Raglan, Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert, was 45 and his mother, Maud de Morley, was 32. He married Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam in 1421, in Bredwardine, Herefordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 5 daughters. In 1442, at the age of 41, his occupation is listed as chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales. He died in 1446, in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales, at the age of 45, and was buried in Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG est né en 1401, à Raglan, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert, avait 45 ans et sa mère, Maud de Morley, avait 32 ans. Il a épousé Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam en 1421, à Bredwardine, Herefordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 5 filles. En 1442, à l’âge de 41 ans, sa profession de son indiquée est chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales. Il est décédé en 1446, à Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, à l’âge de 45 ans, et a été enterré à Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG 1401 in Raglan, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert 45 und seine Mutter Maud de Morley 32. Er hat 1421 in Bredwardine, Herefordshire, England Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 5 Töchter. 1442, als er 41 Jahre alt war, war sein Beruf als chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales aufgeführt. Er starb 1446 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Wales und wurde in Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG è nato il nel 1401 nel Raglan, Monmouthshire, Galles, il suo padre, Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert, aveva 45 anni e il suo madre, Maud de Morley, aveva 32 anni. Lui sposato Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam nel 1421, nel Bredwardine, Herefordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 5 figlie. Nel 1442, all'età di 41 anni, la sua occupazione è indicata come chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales. Morì nel 1446, nel Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Galles, all'età di 45 anni, e fu sepolto a Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KGが1401年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアラグランで生まれた時，父Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbertは45歳，母Maud de Morleyは32歳であった。1421年にイギリスハーエフォードシュアイアブレドワーディンでGwladys ferch Dafydd Gamと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。1442年，41歳の時，職業はchief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in walesと記録されている。彼は1446年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアアバーガベニーで45歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアイスレハムイスレハム プリオリー チャーチにて埋葬された。

ko.Priory Church of St Mary Abergavenny Mon: 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 이슬레함 이슬레함 플리오리 처르치
ko.lifeSummary: Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG(이)가 1401년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 라글런에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert(은)는 45세였고, 그의 어머니 Maud de Morley(은)는 32세였다. 그는 1421년 영국 허퍼드실르 브레드워딘에서 Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 1442년, 41세의 나이로 그의 직업은 chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales이었다. 그는 1446년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 압르가븐이에서 45세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 이슬레함 이슬레함 플리오리 처르치에 묻혔다.

pt.Priory Church of St Mary Abergavenny Mon: Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG nasceu em 1401, em Raglan, Monmouthshire, Gales, seu pai, Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert, tinha 45 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Morley, tinha 32 anos. Ele casou-se com Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam em 1421, em Bredwardine, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 5 filhas. Em 1442, com 41 anos, sua profissão aparece como chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales. Ele faleceu em 1446, em Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales, com 45 anos, e foi sepultado em Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.Priory Church of St Mary Abergavenny Mon: ислэам приори чурч, ислэам, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG родился в 1401 г. в местности раглан, монмоутшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert было 45 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Morley, 32. Он женился на женщине по имени Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam в 1421 г. в местности бредвардаин, хирефордшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 5 дочерей. В 1442 году, в возрасте 41 год,  он работал по профессии chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales. Он умер в 1446 г. в местности абиргавенни, монмоутшир, Уэльс в возрасте 45 лет и был похоронен в местности ислэам приори чурч, ислэам, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.Priory Church of St Mary Abergavenny Mon: Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir William ap Thomas Herbert KG nació en 1401, en Raglan, Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Sir Thomas ap Guillem Herbert, tenía 45 años y su mamá, Maud de Morley, tenía 32 años. Se casó con Gwladys ferch Dafydd Gam en 1421, en Bredwardine, Herefordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 5 hijas. En 1442, a la edad de 41 años, su ocupación registrada era chief steward of richard plantagenet, 3rd duke of york's estates in wales. Murió en 1446, en Abergavenny, Monmouthshire, Gales, a la edad de 45 años, y fue sepultado en Isleham Priory Church, Isleham, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
